_comment: Parallel build lanes as VS Code integrated-terminal tabs. Cmd+Shift+P -> 'Tasks: Run Task' -> pick a lane (or 'Lanes 1-4: open all'). Each opens a dedicated terminal in its worktree and auto-starts the scoped loop. See PARALLEL-BUILD.md. The bootstrap arg is used because a slash command cannot be passed at launch.

tasks.label: Lane 1: Cockpit (R3-PREFS, P7)
args: Invoke the overnight-build skill now and run the autonomous build loop for THIS worktree: read .remember/LANE.md and follow docs/operations/autonomous-build-loop.md sections 15 and 16. Build, gate, adversarially review, run the doc-loop, commit explicit paths with a WHY, fast-forward push. Stay in lane; stop when the queue is dry.

tasks.label: Lane 2: Knowledge (O1, O3)
args: Invoke the overnight-build skill now and run the autonomous build loop for THIS worktree: read .remember/LANE.md and follow docs/operations/autonomous-build-loop.md sections 15 and 16. Build, gate, adversarially review, run the doc-loop, commit explicit paths with a WHY, fast-forward push. Stay in lane; stop when the queue is dry.

tasks.label: Lane 3: Safety (FND-0.5, FND-0.7)
args: Invoke the overnight-build skill now and run the autonomous build loop for THIS worktree: read .remember/LANE.md and follow docs/operations/autonomous-build-loop.md sections 15 and 16. Build, gate, adversarially review, run the doc-loop, commit explicit paths with a WHY, fast-forward push. Stay in lane; stop when the queue is dry.

args: Invoke the overnight-build skill now and run the autonomous build loop for THIS worktree: read .remember/LANE.md and follow docs/operations/autonomous-build-loop.md sections 15 and 16.
